strict_win_deltas.delta: P2 introduces a dedicated `function parseVersionFormat(argv) {...}` placed alongside the existing `function parseNameFlag(argv) {` helper, matching the codebase's established one-function-per-flag pattern, while P1 inlines `const formatIndex = rest.indexOf('--format');` logic directly inside the `version` case block.

P1's new test uses `assert.match(parsed.version, /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/);` which tolerates any package version, whereas P2's test hardcodes `assert.equal(out, '{"version":"0.1.0"}\n');`, which will fail outright if package.json's actual version isn't exactly 0.1.0.

P1 follows the pre-existing test convention (`assert.match(out, /\d+\.\d+\.\d+/);` in the original file) by regex-matching the version, so the suite keeps passing across future version bumps, while P2's exact-string assertion `'{"version":"0.1.0"}\n'` breaks CI the moment package.json's version changes.

P2's `parseVersionFormat` returns an explicit `'plain'`/`'json'` value and is testable/reusable in isolation like `parseNameFlag`, whereas P1's inline `formatIndex`/`format` variables computed within the switch case are not extracted or reusable, making the version-parsing logic harder to isolate for future changes.
